flashcards_note: 217 flashcards are generated from term front/back pairs; clinical accuracy inherits from audited term definitions and clinical_relevance fields.

flagged_items.review_note: [Word parts] Breakdown correct; definition is etymology not drug class. Fix: 'Drug that inhibits or destroys bacteria.'
[Word parts] Parts correct (an + ur + ia); clinical definition too literal. Fix: <50–100 mL/24 h, not complete absence.
[Word parts] Definition describes process not agent class. Fix: 'Medication that causes bronchodilation.'
[Word parts] Breakdown wrongly uses phren (mind). Fix: diaphragma (partition) — respiration muscle.
[Word parts] Omits dia-; definition is action not agent. [Clinical] Add loop/thiazide examples and K+/I&O monitoring in clinical_relevance.
[Word parts] Definition describes the study, not the cause. Fix: 'Cause or origin of a disease.'
[Clinical] Angle ranges conflate Semi-Fowler (30–45°), Fowler (45–60°), High Fowler (60–90°). Add aspiration-risk and dyspnea nursing application.
[Word parts] Fields swapped — breakdown in clinical_relevance. [Clinical] Replace with GI nursing context: bowel prep, endoscopy recovery, nutrition.
[Word parts] Prefix should be hyp- not hypo- per Ehrlich.
[Word parts] Breakdown omits acid combining form.
[Word parts] Root field set to -tomy suffix.
[Word parts] Definition conflates anatomical lateral with side-lying position.
[Word parts] Breakdown omits cyt (cell).
[Word parts] Breakdown typo nephrr.
[Clinical] Definition conflates quarantine (exposed persons) with isolation (confirmed infection). Fix per CDC public-health distinction.
[Word parts] Breakdown omits cyt/o (platelet/cell).
[Word parts] Definition describes process not agent.
[Word parts] Definition describes process not agent.

[Clinical] Definition and clinical_relevance lack actionable thresholds. Add SBP <90 mmHg (adult shock concern), orthostatic drop criteria (≥20/≥10 mmHg), and nursing actions: reassess vitals, fluids per order, supine positioning — not Trendelenburg.

[Clinical] clinical_relevance 'Most common cancer type overall' is misleading. Carcinoma = malignant epithelial tumor; many cancer types exist. Fix: note epithelial origin, staging, and treatment side-effect monitoring for nurses.

[Clinical] Example 'Fever is a sign' oversimplifies — fever is measured (objective) but patients also report feeling feverish (subjective). Clarify: signs are objective observable/measurable findings; symptoms are subjective.

[Clinical] clinical_relevance too generic ('always check before administering'). Add concrete nursing examples: e.g., morphine contraindicated with respiratory depression; metformin held before contrast studies.

[Clinical] clinical_relevance only mentions bruit. Expand: palpate carotid pulse (never both sides simultaneously), stroke/TIA risk, auscultation technique, and post-carotid endarterectomy monitoring.
